user_intent_preservation.original_goal: Migrate 3-phase PWM motor control to new AURIX target
user_intent_preservation.functional_behavior: On TC387 KIT_A2G_TC387_5V_TFT, generate 3-phase complementary, center-aligned PWM on GTM TOM1, Cluster_1 at 20 kHz with 1 µs rising/falling dead-time, using CMU FXCLK0, synchronized start and synchronized shadow updates; map outputs to P02.0/P02.7 (U), P02.1/P02.4 (V), P02.2/P02.5 (W); initialize duties to 25/50/75%; update duties in percent with +10% steps and wrap-to-0-then-add-step behavior; provide a no-op IfxGtm_periodEventFunction; guard GTM enable/clocks if not already enabled; expose initGtmTom3phInv() and updateGtmTom3phInvDuty(); main loop waits 500 ms between updates; include a GTM ATOM ISR (priority 20) that toggles P13.0 (ATOM instance/channel and rate to be finalized).
user_intent_preservation.technical_approach: Use the unified high-level PWM driver IfxGtm_Pwm with TOM backend on Cluster_1. Configure FXCLK0 as the source (100 MHz), frequency 20 kHz, center-aligned mode, complementary outputs with 1 µs dead-time for high/low legs, synchronized start and synchronized shadow updates. Route TOM1 TOUT pins per IfxGtm_PinMap_TC38x_516 mapping for P02.0/P02.7, P02.1/P02.4, P02.2/P02.5. Guard GTM enable and FXCLK0 using IfxGtm_isEnabled/IfxGtm_enable and IfxGtm_Cmu_enableClocks. Initialize U/V/W duty to 25/50/75%. Provide updateGtmTom3phInvDuty() to compute wrap-to-0-then-add-step and push new percents using IfxGtm_Pwm_updateChannelsDutyImmediate. Provide a no-op IfxGtm_periodEventFunction hook. Implement the 500 ms delay with available STM APIs. Provide a basic ATOM interrupt stub at priority 20 that toggles P13.0, to be bound to the user-selected ATOM channel and rate.

driver_selection.selection_rationale: Unified high-level PWM driver covers TOM backend, complementary pairs, center-aligned mode, dead-time insertion, synchronized start and shadow updates in a single configuration, minimizing code and matching the migration goal from IfxGtm_Tom_PwmHl.

runtime_function.name: updateGtmTom3phInvDuty
runtime_function.purpose: Compute 3-phase duty updates in percent with +10% step and wrap-to-0-then-add-step behavior, and apply them atomically using synchronized shadow updates.

driver_changes: IfxGtm_Tom_PwmHl → IfxGtm_Pwm (unified high-level driver with TOM backend)
api_changes: Explicit mode setup via setMode → Configure center-aligned + complementary + dead-time in IfxGtm_Pwm configuration fields
Manual CMU/TOM setup → Guarded GTM enable and FXCLK0 enable via IfxGtm_isEnabled/IfxGtm_enable and IfxGtm_Cmu_enableClocks
Provide IfxGtm_periodEventFunction → Add stubbed no-op function
migration_notes.context_preserved: The unified IfxGtm_Pwm configuration targets TOM1 on Cluster_1, FXCLK0 at 100 MHz, 20 kHz center-aligned complementary PWM with 1 µs dead-time and synchronized start/shadow updates. Duty initialization (25/50/75%) and runtime percent updates using +10% step with wrap-to-0-then-add-step are maintained. Output pins map to P02.[0/7,1/4,2/5] via the TC38x_516 pin map. A GTM ATOM ISR stub (prio 20) is provided to toggle P13.0, pending user selection of ATOM channel/rate. GTM enable and clock activation are guarded to avoid double-init. Note: For the 500 ms delay in the main loop, use available STM timing APIs from IfxStm (e.g., compute ticks using IfxStm_getFrequency and IfxStm_get), since the explicit IfxStm_getTicksFromMilliseconds symbol is not listed in this template.

PWM.rationale: Driver locked by refiner node (user explicit or engine-scored)
